metadata.title: IV RELIFE Dashboard - Production Auth System Audit
metadata.description: Comprehensive audit to ensure Supabase Auth system is production-ready with zero placeholder or mock authentication

audit_objectives.primary_goal: Verify that the Supabase Auth system is fully implemented and production-ready

secondary_goals.goal: Eliminate all placeholder, mock, or demo authentication code
Ensure proper security measures are in place
Confirm session management and security policies
Verify audit logging and monitoring capabilities

section.name: supabase_integration
section.description: Verify Supabase is properly configured and connected

check.description: Confirm all Supabase environment variables are set with real values

validation_criteria.criterion: SUPABASE_URL points to actual Supabase project (not placeholder)
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is valid and not a placeholder
No hardcoded demo URLs or keys remain

check.description: Verify Supabase client is properly initialized

validation_criteria.criterion: No mock client fallback is used in production
Auth configuration includes proper settings (autoRefreshToken, persistSession)
Remove or disable demo/mock authentication paths

section.name: auth_flows
section.description: Ensure all authentication flows use real Supabase Auth

check.description: Verify login uses actual Supabase authentication

validation_criteria.criterion: Remove demo credentials section from LoginForm
Proper error handling for invalid credentials
User data fetched from users table, not mocked

check.description: Verify registration creates real Supabase users

validation_criteria.criterion: User record created in users table
Invite token validation works with real database

check.description: Verify password reset uses Supabase email system
Verify session management uses real Supabase sessions

validation_criteria.criterion: Session expiry based on real Supabase token expiration
Auth state changes handled via supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange

section.name: database_security
section.description: Verify database schema and Row Level Security policies

check.description: Confirm users table matches expected schema

validation_criteria.criterion: users table exists with correct columns (id, email, name, role, retailer_id, location_id)
Role constraint allows only valid roles (owner, backoffice, retailer, location_user)
Proper foreign key relationships to retailers and locations
UUID primary key with proper default

check.description: Verify all auth-related tables exist

validation_criteria.criterion: invite_tokens table exists with proper structure
audit_logs table exists for security logging
outbox table exists for email notifications
All tables have proper constraints and indexes

check.description: Verify Row Level Security policies are implemented

validation_criteria.criterion: RLS enabled on all sensitive tables
Users can only access their own data
Proper policies for invite_tokens and audit_logs

section.name: rbac_implementation
section.description: Verify role-based access control is properly implemented

check.description: Verify AuthGuard components protect routes correctly

validation_criteria.criterion: AuthGuard checks real user authentication status
Role validation uses actual user roles from database

check.description: Verify role-based hooks work with real user data

validation_criteria.criterion: useRole hook checks actual user role from database
hasRole and hasAnyRole functions work correctly
Owner role has proper elevated permissions

check.description: Verify navigation permissions are enforced

validation_criteria.criterion: Navigation items hidden/shown based on real user roles

section.name: security_implementation
section.description: Verify security measures are properly implemented

check.description: Verify session security measures
Verify audit logging captures security events

validation_criteria.criterion: Login events logged to audit_logs table
IP address and user agent captured

check.description: Verify input validation and sanitization

validation_criteria.criterion: Email validation using proper regex/zod schema
Error messages don't leak sensitive information

section.name: user_management
section.description: Verify user migration and invite system work correctly

check.description: Verify user migration handles existing Supabase Auth users

validation_criteria.criterion: Existing Supabase Auth users migrated to users table
No hardcoded user mappings in production

check.description: Verify invite system creates and validates tokens correctly

validation_criteria.criterion: Invite tokens stored in database with expiry
Role and association data properly transferred

section.name: error_handling
section.description: Verify proper error handling and monitoring

check.description: Verify authentication errors are handled gracefully

validation_criteria.criterion: Supabase errors mapped to user-friendly messages
Error states don't expose sensitive information

check.description: Verify monitoring and alerting capabilities

section.name: production_readiness
section.description: Verify system is ready for production deployment

check.description: Ensure all demo/mock code is removed

validation_criteria.criterion: No demo credentials in LoginForm component

check.description: Verify production environment configuration

validation_criteria.criterion: HTTPS enforced for all auth endpoints
Email delivery configured (not using Supabase defaults)

check.description: Verify security headers and policies

test.name: complete_auth_flow
test.description: Test complete authentication flow with real Supabase

steps.step: Register new user with valid email
Verify each role can only access appropriate features
Test session expiry and automatic logout
Test error handling for various scenarios

automated_testing.requirement: Use existing AuthTestRunner component for automated testing
Verify all test scenarios pass with real Supabase
Test error conditions and edge cases

compliance_checklist.item: ✓ All Supabase environment variables configured with real values
✓ No mock or demo authentication code remains
✓ Database schema matches requirements with proper RLS
✓ Session management and security policies active
✓ Audit logging captures all security events
✓ User migration system handles existing users
✓ Invite system creates and validates tokens correctly

action.description: Remove all demo credentials and mock authentication

changes.change: Remove demo credentials section from LoginForm
Remove mock client fallback in supabase-auth.ts
Set SUPABASE_URL to production project URL
Set SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to production anon key

action.description: Implement missing Row Level Security policies

changes.change: Enable RLS on all sensitive tables
Ensure all auth events are logged
Capture IP address and user agent
Log role changes and permission updates

success_criteria.criterion: All authentication flows use real Supabase Auth with no mock fallbacks
Database schema is complete with proper RLS policies
Role-based access control prevents unauthorized access
Session management enforces timeouts and security policies
Audit logging captures all security-relevant events
Error handling is secure and doesn't leak sensitive information
System passes all manual and automated security tests
Production deployment configuration is secure and complete

final_validation.step: Verify all demo/mock code has been removed
Confirm database security policies are active
